mechanism_narrative: A rising Industrial Production Index signals accelerating real output and capacity utilization, which drives corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank Information Coefficient between INDPRO momentum and 20-day S&P 500 returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor income and collapsing aggregate consumption demand, which forces negative earnings revisions and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises while consumer price inflation remains subdued, the resulting increase in the real policy rate tightens financial conditions and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS momentum and CPIAUCSL level exhibits a positive Rank IC with 60-day S&P 500 returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During early-cycle recovery, simultaneous growth in real GDP and retail sales indicates a sustained demand upswing that boosts corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Positive co-movement of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day S&P 500 returns with Rank IC above 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above five percent restricts credit availability and raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0 percent, S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of months over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in nonfarm payrolls indicates strengthening labor demand, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS momentum and 5-day S&P 500 returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint growth in retail sales and housing starts reveals a self-reinforcing demand loop where housing wealth supports consumption, amplifying equity returns beyond the independent effect of either series alone over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a Rank IC above 0.03 with 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a supply-side contraction that precedes broader corporate earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of instances when INDPRO has declined for two consecutive months over any 3-year rolling window.
